title: Multi-thread contact depth doubled across the book this quarter
anchor: Average contacts per account at 4.6 this quarter versus 2.3 last quarter, with 38 of 47 accounts now carrying 4-plus engaged contacts.
connect: Accounts at 4-plus contacts renew at 91% compared to 67% at 1-2 contacts.
body: Average contacts per account at 4.6 this quarter versus 2.3 last quarter, with 38 of 47 accounts now carrying 4-plus engaged contacts. Accounts at 4-plus contacts renew at 91% compared to 67% at 1-2 contacts.
trace: Multi-threading signal shows dramatic improvement in relationship depth with strong renewal correlation data.

title: Champion re-engagement converted 7 of 9 accounts back to green within 21 days
anchor: April champion re-engagement targeted 9 accounts where contacts had gone 14-plus days silent; all received confirmed responses.
connect: March champion re-engagement volume was 3 accounts total.
body: April champion re-engagement targeted 9 accounts where contacts had gone 14-plus days silent; all received confirmed responses. March champion re-engagement volume was 3 accounts total.
trace: Champion outreach effectiveness shows both increased volume and conversion rate to green status.

title: 44 of 47 accounts show CSM touch within the past two weeks
anchor: Current two-week outreach coverage sits at the highest level since Q4 2025.
connect: This represents 94% of the active book with recent CSM interaction.
body: Current two-week outreach coverage sits at the highest level since Q4 2025. This represents 94% of the active book with recent CSM interaction.
trace: Outreach cadence data shows consistent engagement across nearly the entire book.

title: Strategic-tier stakeholder maps hit 84% freshness in April
anchor: 21 of 25 strategic accounts updated stakeholder maps within the last 30 days, up from 14 of 25 in February.
connect: Strategic accounts with fresh stakeholder maps renew 22 points higher than those without.
body: 21 of 25 strategic accounts updated stakeholder maps within the last 30 days, up from 14 of 25 in February. Strategic accounts with fresh stakeholder maps renew 22 points higher than those without.
trace: Stakeholder map currency shows significant improvement with strong renewal correlation for strategic tier.

title: Q3 renewal pipeline carries $1.8M across 18 accounts
anchor: Executive sponsor touch logged in last 30 days for 14 of 18 renewal accounts.
connect: Renewals with executive engagement in the 90 days before close at 89% versus 61% without.
body: Executive sponsor touch logged in last 30 days for 14 of 18 renewal accounts. Renewals with executive engagement in the 90 days before close at 89% versus 61% without.
trace: Q3 renewal pipeline shows strong executive engagement with clear correlation to close rates.

title: 6 accounts pulled renewal forward to Q2 for $420K combined ARR
anchor: 5 of 6 early-renewal accounts had been flagged green for two consecutive months before the early-commit conversation.
connect: These accounts were originally tracking to Q3 or Q4 renewal windows.
body: 5 of 6 early-renewal accounts had been flagged green for two consecutive months before the early-commit conversation. These accounts were originally tracking to Q3 or Q4 renewal windows.
trace: Early renewal signal shows strong correlation between consistent green status and willingness to commit early.

title: Mid-market GRR improved 7 points to 94% in Q2
anchor: 31 of 33 mid-market accounts renewed in Q2 versus 28 of 33 in Q1.
connect: ICP-aligned mid-market accounts carried 86% of the Q2 improvement.
body: 31 of 33 mid-market accounts renewed in Q2 versus 28 of 33 in Q1. ICP-aligned mid-market accounts carried 86% of the Q2 improvement.
trace: Mid-market retention improvement concentrates in ICP-aligned accounts suggesting fit correlation.

title: 7 accounts crossed 80% feature adoption ceiling in April
anchor: Combined expansion potential sits at $290K based on next-tier list pricing.
connect: 4 of 7 carry a logged executive sponsor on the expansion conversation track.
body: Combined expansion potential sits at $290K based on next-tier list pricing. 4 of 7 carry a logged executive sponsor on the expansion conversation track.
trace: Feature adoption ceiling signal shows clear expansion readiness with executive engagement already initiated.

title: 12 accounts now carry expansion-ready multi-thread depth at 5-plus contacts
anchor: Up from 4 accounts last quarter at the 5-plus contact threshold.
connect: Accounts at 5-plus contacts expand at 30% in the next 12 months compared to 5% at single-thread.
body: Up from 4 accounts last quarter at the 5-plus contact threshold. Accounts at 5-plus contacts expand at 30% in the next 12 months compared to 5% at single-thread.
trace: Expansion-depth multi-threading shows significant growth with strong conversion correlation.

title: 8 accounts hit usage spike threshold in the past 14 days

connect: Usage spikes preceding expansion conversations correlate with 33% faster close-cycle.
body: Usage spikes defined as 40% week-over-week increase; 6 of 8 carry active expansion tracks. Usage spikes preceding expansion conversations correlate with 33% faster close-cycle.
trace: Recent usage spike signals show strong expansion track conversion and velocity correlation.

title: Strategic QBR completion hit 88% with value stories captured

connect: February QBR completion with value stories was 64%.
body: 22 of 25 strategic-tier QBRs completed in April with structured value summaries logged in CRM. February QBR completion with value stories was 64%.
trace: QBR completion and value story capture both improved significantly over the quarter.

title: QBRs with value summaries correlate with 18-point higher renewal commits

connect: This correlation spans both strategic and mid-market tiers in the historical data.
body: Structured value documentation shows impact on renewal conversation outcomes in the 90 days that follow QBR completion. This correlation spans both strategic and mid-market tiers in the historical data.
trace: Value story capture shows measurable impact on renewal commitment rates across segments.

title: Time to first value dropped to 41 days median in Q2
anchor: Q2 onboarding cohort of 11 accounts hit first activated success criterion at 41 days versus 67 days for 9 Q1 accounts.
connect: Accounts hitting first value within 45 days renew 26 points higher.
body: Q2 onboarding cohort of 11 accounts hit first activated success criterion at 41 days versus 67 days for 9 Q1 accounts. Accounts hitting first value within 45 days renew 26 points higher.
trace: Onboarding velocity improvement shows significant impact with clear renewal correlation threshold.

title: Handoff completeness improved to 79% of Q2 closed-won deals

connect: Q1 handoff completeness was 11 of 27 deals at 41%.
body: 19 of 24 Q2 deals carried full stakeholder map, buying reason, and verbal commitments at CSM handoff. Q1 handoff completeness was 11 of 27 deals at 41%.

title: Complete handoffs accelerate first value by 19 days

body: Onboardings with full stakeholder context hit activated success criterion 19 days faster than those without. This correlation holds across both mid-market and strategic account tiers.
trace: Handoff completeness shows measurable impact on onboarding velocity across account segments.
